Subject: Analysis Questions
Question: True or False? If (b_n) coverges to b then |b_n| converges to |b|.
Response1:
Final Answer: True
Explanation: If (b_n) converges to b then for any ε > 0 there exists an N such that |b_n - b| < ε. Taking absolute values on both sides gives |b_n| - |b| ≤ |b_n - b|. By the triangle inequality, |b_n - b| ≤ |b_n| + |b|. Thus, combining these inequalities yields |b_n| - |b| ≤ 2ε for all n > N. Since ε is arbitrary, this shows that (|b_n|) converges to |b|.
